title: Joins, Cardinality, Unmatched Rows, and Fanout Control · Data-System Defense
summary: Ticket, team, owner, and event data must be combined without losing unassigned work or multiplying effort totals. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a cardinality-safe support investigation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.
Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
Model and query many-to-many relationships through an associative relation with its own grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inner Join · changed case · Quiz Inner Join
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 3 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. Competency probe: Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.

Which action demonstrates Inner Join without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Inner Join · changed case · Quiz Inner Join.
